sheet.title: VFD Soft-Start Sequence — 22 kW HVAC Chilled-Water Pump P1

items.rating: 63 A 3-pole manual lockable isolator (upstream of VFD)
63 A AC-1 line contactor (VFD line-side, NEVER switches under load)
Class 10A thermal — VFD-internal overload (handled by VFD I²t algorithm, sketched as F1 symbol for completeness)

Variable Frequency Drive — 22 kW 50 A continuous, 4-20 mA speed-ref input + 24 VDC run-permissive + at-speed digital output (signal_converter device_class captures the analogue-control I/O block per generator prompt v1.0 enum policy)
22 kW / 400 V / 42 A FLA / 3-phase induction (chilled-water pump P1)
Soft-start ramp 3 s on-delay (0 Hz → 50 Hz over 3 s)
STOP — red flush actuator, NC 21-22 in coil-break path
Incoming power terminal block L1/L2/L3 + PE, 16 mm² DIN-rail
Motor-tail outgoing terminal block U/V/W + PE, 10 mm² DIN-rail (VFD-output side, must be screened cable to motor)
Control supply L/N 230 VAC + 24 VDC + BMS I/O (4-20 mA speed-ref + alarm out)

connections.function: Incoming L1 to isolator pole 1
Incoming L2 to isolator pole 2
Incoming L3 to isolator pole 3
Isolated L1 to line contactor pole 1
Isolated L2 to K1 pole 2
Isolated L3 to K1 pole 3
K1 output L1 to VFD line input L1
K1 output L2 to VFD line input L2
K1 output L3 to VFD line input L3
VFD output U to motor-tail terminal U (screened cable required)
VFD output V to motor-tail terminal V
VFD output W to motor-tail terminal W
Motor-tail U to motor M1 terminal U
Motor-tail V to M1 terminal V
Motor-tail W to M1 terminal W
Control L to STOP NC contact 21
STOP 22 to START NO 13
START 14 to K1 coil A1 (line-contactor energise)
K1 coil A2 return to control N
Hold-in: STOP 22 to K1-AUX 13
K1-AUX 14 holds K1 coil after S1 release
K1-AUX also drives VFD run-permissive 24 VDC input (line contactor proven closed)
Run-permissive also starts soft-start ramp timer KT1
KT1 coil return to 24 VDC common
F1 95-96 NC trip-contact in K1 coil-break path (overload trip drops K1)
F1 96 return to N (closes the F1-in-coil-break loop)
BMS 4-20 mA speed-reference + into VFD analogue input AI1
BMS speed-reference return
VFD digital output RAMPING (24 VDC during 0-3 s acceleration) drives H2 amber lamp
H2 return to 24 VDC common
VFD digital output AT-SPEED (24 VDC at ≥95% of speed-ref) drives H1 green RUN lamp
H1 return to 24 VDC common
VFD fault digital output drives H3 red lamp
H3 return to 24 VDC common

labels.text: VFD modelled as signal_converter device_class — captures analogue + digital I/O block per generator prompt v1.0 enum
AI1+ / AI1- (BMS speed-ref) / RUN-PERMIT / DO-RAMPING / DO-AT-SPEED / DO-FAULT
Line contactor K1 NEVER switches under load — only isolates the VFD line side. Motor torque is built/released by VFD output, not by K1.
Screened cable mandatory between VFD output and motor — limits dV/dt-driven common-mode currents and meets IEC 61800-3 EMC

sequence_of_operation: ENABLE: Operator presses S1 → S2 22 (NC, closed) + S1 14 (NO, closed) energise K1 coil A1-A2 → K1 closes line poles + K1-AUX 13/14 closes → K1-AUX provides hold-in + drives VFD RUN-PERMIT input 24 VDC. STATE 1 SOFT-START RAMP (t = 0 → 3 s): RUN-PERMIT energises KT1 (3 s on-delay). VFD begins frequency ramp from 0 Hz to BMS-commanded reference frequency (typically 50 Hz, but driven by BMS 4-20 mA via AI1) over 3 s. Motor M1 current ramps gradually from 0 A to 42 A FLA instead of seeing inrush 7×FLA. VFD asserts DO-RAMPING 24 VDC → H2 amber lit. STATE 2 RUN-PERMISSIVE (t = 3 s): KT1 times out (informational — the actual at-speed signal is VFD-internal). VFD reaches ≥95% of reference frequency → VFD asserts DO-AT-SPEED 24 VDC → H1 green lit, H2 extinguishes. VFD now in closed-loop speed control, tracking BMS 4-20 mA reference. STATE 3 STEADY-STATE (t = 3 s + ∞): VFD modulates motor frequency continuously per BMS-commanded reference. Motor current tracks pump load curve (typically 0.6-1.0×FLA on a part-load CHW pump). STOP RAMP-DOWN: Operator presses S2 → S2 21-22 opens → K1 coil drops → K1-AUX 13/14 opens → VFD RUN-PERMIT 24 VDC removed → VFD begins controlled deceleration (manufacturer-default 5 s decel) → at 0 Hz VFD coasts. K1 main poles open at the line side (no current at this point because VFD has internally crow-barred output). FAULT: VFD-internal overload (I²t algorithm sketched as F1 thermal symbol for completeness) OR over-voltage OR over-current OR PTC sensor OR communication-loss latches VFD trip → DO-FAULT 24 VDC → H3 red lit. F1 95-96 NC opens K1 coil-break path → K1 drops out → motor coasts. Reset requires VFD HMI fault clear before re-START.

assumptions: VFD modelled with device_class signal_converter — the v1.0 schematic IR enum has no dedicated 'vfd' or 'inverter' class; signal_converter is the closest fit per the generator prompt v1.0 enum-mapping policy and captures the VFD's analogue + digital I/O block.

IEC 60364-5-55 § 559 governs electrical equipment for variable-speed drives; IEC 60364-4-43 § 433 governs overload protection (handled by VFD I²t, not by external thermal); IEC 61800-3 governs EMC compliance for power drive systems and mandates screened motor cables.

Upstream rating consumed from db-layout-rollup (DB-MECH 63 A circuit) and prospective fault current consumed from fault-level (8.2 kA at DB-MECH outgoing) — both intents recorded in meta.consumed_intents[].
Soft-start ramp 3 s selected for HVAC chilled-water pump — long enough to avoid water-hammer in the chilled-water loop, short enough to not stall on pump curve start-up torque demand.

No earthing intent consumed; TT or TN-C-S assumed engineer-declared via inputs.json — VFD requires PE conductor connected to drive enclosure earthing terminal for safe operation, irrespective of earthing system.

F1 overload symbol is drawn on the schematic for documentation completeness but the actual thermal-overload function is performed by the VFD's internal I²t algorithm — the F1 95-96 contact represents the VFD's overload-trip auxiliary output.

rationale.chat_summary: VFD soft-start sequence for a 22 kW HVAC chilled-water pump P1, IEC/INT jurisdiction. VFD1 (signal_converter device_class per v1.0 enum) ramps 0→50 Hz over 3 s via KT1 + BMS 4-20 mA speed-ref; K1 line contactor + Q1 isolator + VFD-internal Class 10A I²t overload + 3 indicator lamps (H1 AT-SPEED green, H2 RAMPING amber, H3 FAULT red). Consumes db-layout-rollup + fault-level. Reply to refine (e.g. add BMS-trip-on-comms-loss).

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: control_sequence (VFD_soft_start topology) on sheet SHT-003 for an IEC/INT-jurisdiction commercial HVAC project. Subject is P1, a 22 kW / 400 V / 42 A FLA chilled-water pump driven by a VFD-controlled 3-phase induction motor. The schematic consumes two intents: db-layout-rollup (DB-MECH 63 A outgoing circuit) and fault-level (8.2 kA prospective at DB-MECH outgoing). No earthing intent available — TT or TN-C-S assumed and disclosed in compliance_summary.assumptions[]. The VFD itself is modelled with device_class signal_converter, the closest fit in the v1.0 IR enum which has no dedicated vfd/inverter class.

decisions.label: VFD soft-start topology for 22 kW HVAC pump
decisions.summary: Soft-start ramp 0→50 Hz over 3 s avoids the 7×FLA inrush and water-hammer associated with DOL on a chilled-water loop.

decisions.label: VFD modelled as signal_converter device_class
decisions.summary: v1.0 IR enum has no vfd/inverter class — signal_converter captures the analogue + digital I/O block; the documentation choice is disclosed in labels[] and assumptions[].
decisions.rule: Out-of-enum device class mapping per generator prompt

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: Upstream: DB-MECH outgoing 63 A 3-phase circuit, 400 V supply, 8.2 kA prospective fault current. db-layout-rollup intent supplied the 63 A circuit rating that drives Q1/K1 selection at 63 A; fault-level intent supplied the 8.2 kA pfc that confirms the VFD's declared 50 kA SCCR (short-circuit current rating per IEC 61439-2 Annex 101) provides >6× headroom. The VFD line-side line contactor K1 is AC-1 utilisation (steady-state switching only) because the motor torque is built and released by the VFD output, never by K1 itself — K1 never switches under load.

decisions.label: Upstream 63 A consumed from db-layout-rollup
decisions.summary: DB-MECH outgoing 63 A circuit sizes Q1/K1 selection.

decisions.label: Prospective fault 8.2 kA consumed from fault-level
decisions.summary: VFD declared SCCR 50 kA covers 8.2 kA with >6× headroom; line contactor K1 short-time withstand also exceeds 8.2 kA.

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 15 devices placed. Power: Q1 (63 A 3-pole manual lockable isolator), K1 (63 A AC-1 line contactor + K1-AUX hold), F1 (Class 10A thermal — actually VFD-internal I²t with the symbol drawn for documentation completeness), VFD1 (22 kW 50 A drive), M1 (22 kW 42 A pump motor). Control: KT1 (3 s soft-start ramp on-delay), S1 START, S2 STOP. Indication: H1 green (AT-SPEED), H2 amber (RAMPING 0-3 s), H3 red (FAULT). 3 terminal rails X1/X2/X3 segregate incoming power, screened motor-cable outgoing power, and the mixed control supply (230 VAC + 24 VDC + BMS 4-20 mA I/O).

decisions.label: K1 AC-1 line contactor (NEVER switches under load)
decisions.summary: Motor torque is built/released by the VFD output, not by K1 — K1 is therefore correctly sized for AC-1 (steady-state) and not AC-3 (motor switching).
decisions.rule: Contactor utilisation category for VFD line-side
decisions.code_clause: IEC 60947-4-1 § 4.4.2 (AC-1) + IEC 60947-4-2 (semiconductor controllers — line contactor not switching under load)

decisions.label: F1 symbol drawn but function is VFD-internal I²t
decisions.summary: F1 95-96 NC contact represents the VFD's overload-trip auxiliary output; the actual thermal model lives in the VFD firmware.

decisions.label: Screened cable mandatory VFD → motor (X2 → M1)
decisions.summary: IEC 61800-3 mandates screened motor cables to manage common-mode currents driven by VFD dV/dt PWM switching.
decisions.rule: EMC requirement for power drive systems
decisions.code_clause: IEC 61800-3 (EMC for adjustable-speed electrical power drive systems)

sections.title: Connection Topology
sections.summary: Power (W1-W15, power_LV, 16 mm² Cu line + 10 mm² Cu screened motor cable): X1 → Q1 → K1 → VFD1 → X2 → M1. Note that the conductor csa drops from 16 to 10 mm² between VFD input and motor — sized against VFD continuous output (50 A) rather than the line-side rating (63 A). Control (W16-W21, control_LV, 1.5 mm² Cu, 230 VAC): canonical 3-wire S2-NC → S1-NO → K1 coil with K1-AUX hold. Auxiliary 24 VDC (W22-W34, aux_DC, 1.5 mm² Cu): K1-AUX 14 asserts VFD RUN-PERMIT + KT1 ramp timer; BMS 4-20 mA speed-ref on AI1; VFD digital outputs DO-RAMPING/DO-AT-SPEED/DO-FAULT drive the three indicator lamps via X3 24 VDC common. The F1 95-96 path (W25-W26) loops the VFD-internal overload trip back into the K1 coil-break path so that a VFD-detected overload also drops the upstream line contactor.

decisions.label: VFD output cable smaller than line-side cable
decisions.summary: Motor cable sized for VFD continuous output 50 A — line side carries the same active power at slightly higher (~10%) rms due to VFD harmonics, hence 16 mm² line / 10 mm² output.
decisions.rule: Conductor sizing for VFD-driven motor circuits

decisions.label: VFD-internal overload loops back to K1 coil
decisions.summary: F1 95-96 NC in K1 coil-break path — VFD-detected overload drops both VFD and line contactor; coil-break placed in series with S2-NC for canonical fail-safe.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against IEC 60364-5-55 § 559 (variable-speed drives), IEC 60364-4-43 § 433 (overload protection), IEC 60364-5-52 (conductors), IEC 60947-4-1 (LV switchgear — contactors), IEC 60947-4-2 (semiconductor motor controllers), IEC 61800-3 (EMC for power drive systems), IEC 61439-2 Annex 101 (SCCR for type-tested assemblies), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-12 (symbols for logic and switching functions). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Six assumptions documented including the signal_converter device-class mapping choice and the F1 representational drawing.

decisions.label: IEC-jurisdiction primary citation form
decisions.summary: No BS 7671 or NEC references appear — citations are IEC throughout, per jurisdiction routing.

decisions.label: IEC 60947-4-2 for VFD line contactor
decisions.summary: Semiconductor controller standard distinguishes line-contactor + overload duty when the actual motor switching is done by the semiconductor stack (VFD).

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Hybrid-mode: db-layout-rollup + fault-level consumed; earthing intent absent (TT or TN-C-S engineer-declared in inputs.json). VFD soft-start ramp 3 s selected for the chilled-water loop — long enough to avoid water-hammer, short enough to not stall the pump under start-up torque demand. BMS speed-reference is a 4-20 mA loop on AI1 (4 mA = 0 Hz, 20 mA = 50 Hz) — typical BMS speed control via a Modbus-RTU or BACnet bridge that converts the BMS frequency command to the analogue signal. Screened cable between VFD and motor (X2 → M1) is non-negotiable per IEC 61800-3 — unscreened cable drives common-mode bearing currents and external EMC nuisance on nearby instrumentation.

decisions.label: Hybrid-mode: 2 intents consumed, 1 absent
decisions.rule: Hybrid-mode disclosure per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Cross-Skill Cascade Verification
sections.summary: db-layout-rollup → schematic: DB-MECH 63 A outgoing circuit rating drives Q1/K1 selection at 63 A AC-1. fault-level → schematic: 8.2 kA prospective fault at DB-MECH outgoing verifies VFD declared SCCR 50 kA (>6× headroom) and K1 short-time withstand. Cascade is self-consistent: 63 A db-layout circuit ↔ 63 A Q1 ↔ 63 A K1 ↔ 50 A VFD continuous ↔ 42 A motor FLA. The conductor csa step from 16 to 10 mm² between line and motor reflects the rms-current step from line-side (~50 A worst-case with harmonics) to VFD-output (50 A continuous). When earthing intent is added in a future revision, the VFD enclosure PE bond and motor PE conductor + screened-cable termination should be re-verified.

decisions.label: Two-intent cascade verified
decisions.summary: 63 A circuit ↔ 50 A VFD ↔ 42 A motor; 8.2 kA fault ↔ 50 kA SCCR.
